behavior_summary: Provides interaction and schedule management for the NPC Mordea. It implements the 'U8P_MORDEA::look' (inspect) response that composes and emits name/title fragments, a schedule driver that sets Mordea's daily state and coordinates schedules for Aramina and Salkind, fast-area entry/exit handlers that dispatch scheduled behaviors when the Avatar crosses Mordea's trigger region, and a theft-response hook for when the Avatar steals items.

MORDEA::look composes short text fragments (full name or combinations of short name, job/title, and the word 'woman') based on two global flags (mordeaName, mordeaJob) and directs those fragments to the inspecting process via Item::bark.

MORDEA::schedule guards against story/dead-state conditions (toranDead, secondexecution, Npc::isDead), queries a time/calendar service (FREE::28F9) to select a schedule, resets reference/time slots (resetRef), writes mordeaSchedule and related schedule globals for neighboring NPCs, and spawns asynchronous behavior methods selected by time/map checks.

enterFastArea/leaveFastArea are trigger handlers for the Avatar entering/leaving Mordea's fast trigger area: enterFastArea re-checks guards, resets refs, and spawns schedule-specific fast-entry behavior (based on mordeaSchedule); leaveFastArea cleans the same refs.

AvatarStoleSomething reacts to theft events: it tests the stolen item's type (Item::getType) and Mordea's current schedule (observed branch when mordeaSchedule==3) and, if conditions match, resets refs and initiates a reaction behavior.
notable_control_flow: MORDEA::look: conditional branching on two globals mordeaName and mordeaJob to choose which set of Item::bark calls to perform (full name vs. component fragments).

U8P_MORDEA::schedule: early-exit if toranDead or secondexecution true or if Npc::isDead(pid) returns true; otherwise call FREE::28F9 repeatedly to select time-based branches, reset multiple refs, write mordeaSchedule (and araminaSchedule/SalkindSchedule), then spawn external methods conditionally depending on returned time and Npc::getMap(pid).

U8P_MORDEA::enterFastArea: same story/dead guards as schedule(), reset refs, then switch on mordeaSchedule to spawn schedule-specific fast-area handlers (notably branches for schedule==2 and schedule==3).
U8P_MORDEA::leaveFastArea: non-branching cleanup that calls resetRef on a set of reference slots to clear transient schedule/location state.
U8P_MORDEA::AvatarStoleSomething: checks stolen item type and mordeaSchedule; when matching (observed for schedule==3) clears refs and triggers a response (spawn or similar).
open_questions: Exact runtime semantics and return value meanings of FREE::28F9 (appears to be time-of-day or day-phase service) — mapping of its numeric returns to concrete times is not present.

Concrete behavior implemented by the spawned METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::15E7 and other METHOD:: IDs — these are referenced by spawn but their source/semantics are outside this script excerpt.
Precise meaning and scope of the reference slots cleared by resetRef (indices used) — whether they represent waypoints, timers, or linkages to other processes is inferred but not explicit.
Whether mordeaJob and mordeaName are mutually exclusive configuration flags or can both be set simultaneously in live data (U8P_MORDEA::look() handles both cases but intent is inferred).

main_function.name: U8P_MORDEA::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an Avatar 'U8P_MORDEA::look' (inspect) event on the Mordea NPC by composing and emitting appropriate name/title text fragments to the inspecting process. The routine consults mordeaName and mordeaJob to choose full vs. component strings and calls Item::bark to display/speak them.
entry_conditions: Invoked with the inspecting process/player PID passed as the process parameter.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEA::schedule
function_map.role: Set Mordea's daily/behavior schedule: guard against story/dead flags, query time service (FREE::28F9), reset refs, write schedule globals (mordeaSchedule, araminaSchedule, SalkindSchedule), and spawn asynchronous movement/behavior methods depending on time/map.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEA::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger: when the Avatar enters Mordea's trigger region, guard by story/dead flags, reset refs, read mordeaSchedule and spawn the schedule-appropriate fast-entry behavior (distinct branches for schedule values such as 2 and 3).

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEA::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger: cleanup/reset the same reference/time slots used by schedule/enterFastArea to stop or clear transient behaviors when Avatar leaves the region.

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEA::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: Theft-response handler: triggered when the Avatar steals an item (Event 13); tests the stolen item's type and mordeaSchedule and triggers a response when conditions match (observed branch for mordeaSchedule==3).

function_map.name: U8P_MORDEA::look
function_map.role: Primary interaction entry for 'U8P_MORDEA::look' event; composes and barks name/title fragments based on mordeaName and mordeaJob flags.

external_calls.purpose: Emit/display short textual fragments (NPC full name, short name, title, 'woman') to the inspecting process/player.
Guard: skip scheduling if Mordea is dead.
Query a global time/calendar service to select schedule branches based on time/day-phase.
Clear per-NPC reference/time slots (reset locations, timers, or linked process refs) before assigning new schedule state.
Start asynchronous behavior/movement methods (METHOD::143A, METHOD::133F, METHOD::15E7 etc.) corresponding to the chosen schedule/time branch.
Query Mordea's current map id to decide map-specific schedule/behavior spawning.
Clear the same transient refs/timers when the Avatar enters the trigger before spawning schedule-specific fast-entry behaviors.
Spawn a fast-entry behavior method selected by mordeaSchedule (distinct handlers for schedule values).
Cleanup transient refs/timers on Avatar exit from Mordea's trigger region.
Determine the stolen item's type to decide whether Mordea should react.
Clear refs/timers before triggering a theft reaction.

reads.name: mordeaName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether MORDEA::look uses an extended/full name or falls back to short name fragments.

reads.name: mordeaJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether MORDEA::look includes a job/title fragment when composing the U8P_MORDEA::look output.

reads.name: toranDead
reads.likely_effect: Story/dead-state guard: when true schedule and fast-area behaviors are skipped to avoid running NPC behavior in that story state.

reads.name: secondexecution
reads.likely_effect: Additional story guard used alongside toranDead to inhibit scheduling/behaviors under specific progression conditions.

reads.name: mordeaSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Numeric schedule state (1,2,3,...) that drives which behavior/spawn branches are used on scheduling and fast-area entry.

reads.name: araminaSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Written to by schedule() to coordinate Aramina's schedule with Mordea; likely read by Aramina's own scripts.

reads.name: SalkindSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Written to by schedule() to coordinate Salkind's schedule with Mordea; likely read by Salkind's scripts.

writes.name: mordeaSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Set by U8P_MORDEA::schedule to a chosen numeric schedule state that downstream handlers (enterFastArea, theft reaction) read to determine behavior.

writes.name: araminaSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Updated alongside mordeaSchedule to synchronize Aramina's activity schedule with Mordea's decisions.

writes.name: SalkindSchedule
writes.likely_effect: Updated alongside mordeaSchedule to synchronize Salkind's activity schedule with Mordea's decisions.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides interaction and schedule management for the NPC Mordea. It implements the 'U8P_MORDEA::look' (inspect) response that composes and emits name/title fragments, a schedule driver that sets Mordea's daily state and coordinates schedules for Aramina and Salkind, fast-area entry/exit handlers that dispatch scheduled behaviors when the Avatar crosses Mordea's trigger region, and a theft-response hook for when the Avatar steals items.
